title: say-it session_state
description: Within-session runtime state: the values that change as one empty-chair session runs (current stage, turn count, extension flag). Owned by the per-turn hook tick (ADR 0004), NOT by the conversation context. The `active` flag is the gate that tells the globally-firing UserPromptSubmit hook whether a say-it session is in progress. Single live file at <data_dir>/session_state.json.

active.description: True while a /say-it session is running. The hook ticks and injects state only when this is true; otherwise it stays silent. Set true by session_start, false by session_end/closure.

session_id.description: Claude Code session id this state belongs to (from the hook event).

persona_id.description: Which persona this session is about (matches personas/<id>.json).

theme_label.description: The (persona, grievance-theme) issue label. Null until closure (label is confirmed and the takeaway is saved at the exit, per 'compare at entry, store at exit'). Used by the revisit guard.

stage.description: The empty-chair arc stage. S1 vent -> S2 role-swap -> S3 integration -> S4 closure.

turn.description: User turns counted in this session. Incremented +1 by the hook tick every user message.

extension_used.description: Whether the one-time (+3 turn) extension past the soft cap has been spent. Enforcement lives in issue 04; this flag is the deterministic record.

blocked.description: Terminal safety hold latched by the distress circuit-breaker on an acute-harm (Grade 2) signal (ADR 0003, issue 07). True = the session was stopped for acute self-harm and will NOT resume: the tick hook re-injects a hold instead of ticking, and session_start refuses to open a new round. Set by the hook tick (HARD floor) or session_block.py (SOFT layer). Distinct from the persona-level block flag (issue 08). Defaults false; older session files without it read as not-blocked.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last tick.

guards.description: Per-turn guard results computed by the hook. Distress detection runs the HARD-floor keyword regexes (issue 07; patterns/hotline in lexicon/distress.ko.json) and routes panic/acute-harm; turn-cap soft/hard flags are computed deterministically (issue 04).

tier.description: null when clear. 'panic' -> stop + de-escalate; 'acute-harm' -> stop + crisis hotline + no resume (ADR 0003).
